botox.title: Botox
botox.desc: Smooth fine lines and reduce wrinkles with Botox treatments in Newport and Portsmouth, RI. Schedule a consultation today.

blocks.x: How Botox Works

Botox contains a purified protein that temporarily relaxes specific facial muscles. When injected in precise locations, it prevents those muscles from contracting fully. The lines and wrinkles caused by repeated muscle movements gradually soften and smooth over the following days and weeks.

Your facial expressions create dynamic wrinkles that appear when you smile, frown, or squint. Over the years of making these expressions, wrinkles appear even when your face rests in a neutral position. Botox addresses the active wrinkles that show during expressions and the static lines that remain visible at rest.

The treatment works predictably and reversibly. Effects develop over three to seven days and reach full strength around two weeks post-treatment. Results typically last three to four months before muscle activity gradually returns. Regular treatments maintain your results, and many patients find their results last progressively longer with consistent use.
Why Dentists Excel at Botox

You might wonder why your dental practice offers Botox. The answer lies in specialized training. Dentists study facial anatomy extensively—far more than most medical professionals. They work in and around facial structures daily, developing an intimate understanding of muscle relationships, nerve pathways, and aesthetic proportions.

We know exactly where to place injections for optimal results and maximum safety. We understand how facial muscles interact and can predict how treating one area affects surrounding regions. This expertise translates to natural-looking results that enhance rather than freeze your features.
Cosmetic Applications

Botox in Newport effectively treats the most common signs of facial aging. Forehead lines that make you look worried or tired smooth away within days of treatment. Crow’s feet—those lines radiating from the outer corners of your eyes—soften significantly. The vertical lines between your eyebrows that create an angry or stressed appearance relax into a more peaceful expression.
Popular Treatment Areas Include:

Skilled injectors like our dental team create balanced, harmonious results. They treat isolated wrinkles and consider your entire face and how different areas work together. This comprehensive approach prevents the overdone, frozen look that gives Botox a bad reputation when administered incorrectly.
TMJ Disorder Relief

Temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders cause jaw pain, clicking sounds, difficulty chewing, and chronic headaches. Many patients suffer for years, trying various treatments with limited success. Botox offers remarkable relief by relaxing the overactive jaw muscles responsible for TMJ symptoms.

The masseter muscles—your primary chewing muscles—often become enlarged and overworked in TMJ patients. They create tension, pain, and sometimes visible jaw squaring, affecting facial aesthetics. Botox injections relax these muscles, reducing pain and stress while creating a slimmer jawline as a pleasant side effect.

Treatment typically involves injections into the masseter muscles and sometimes the temporalis muscles at your temples. Patients report significant pain reduction within a week or two. The relief lasts several months, and regular treatments can break the cycle of chronic jaw tension that perpetuates TMJ problems.
Headache and Migraine Treatment

Chronic tension headaches rob you of productivity and enjoyment. Many sufferers don’t realize Botox can provide substantial relief. The treatment works by relaxing muscles that contribute to tension headaches and by potentially blocking pain signals before they reach nerve endings.

The FDA specifically approved Botox for chronic migraine treatment in patients who experience headaches 15 or more days per month. Treatment involves multiple injection points across the forehead, temples, back of the head, and neck. Many patients experience dramatic reductions in both headache frequency and intensity.

Dr. Pannes can evaluate whether your headache pattern makes you a good candidate for Botox therapy. When properly documented and prescribed, this therapeutic application often qualifies for medical insurance coverage.
Your Treatment Experience

Botox appointments typically take 10 to 20 minutes. We’ll discuss your concerns and goals, then mark the planned injection sites. The actual injections feel like small pinpricks—most patients tolerate them easily without numbing. Some areas prove more sensitive than others, but discomfort remains minimal and brief.

You can return to normal activities immediately after treatment. To prevent product migration, we recommend avoiding strenuous exercise for a few hours and staying upright for several hours. Don’t massage or apply pressure to treated areas for at least 24 hours.

Results appear gradually. You’ll notice initial improvements within three to five days, with full effects visible around two weeks. We’ll schedule a follow-up appointment to assess your results and make any necessary touch-up adjustments. This ensures optimal outcomes, especially for first-time patients.
Safety and Side Effects

Botox has an exceptional safety record when administered by trained professionals. Millions of treatments occur annually with very few complications. Side effects typically remain minor and temporary—slight bruising at injection sites, mild headache, or temporary eyelid drooping in rare cases.

Choosing an experienced injector dramatically reduces risk. Our dental team’s anatomical expertise and steady hands ensure precise placement every time. We use only genuine Botox from Allergan—never generic alternatives or questionable products that might compromise your safety.

Certain medical conditions and medications contraindicate Botox treatment. We’ll review your medical history thoroughly before proceeding. Pregnant or nursing women should postpone Botox until after this period. People with neuromuscular disorders require special consideration and medical clearance.
Maintaining Your Results

Most patients return every three to four months to maintain their results. Some areas last longer than others—crow’s feet often need refreshing sooner than forehead lines. Developing a regular treatment schedule prevents wrinkles from fully returning and usually allows for smaller maintenance doses over time.

Combining Botox with good skincare amplifies and extends your results. Daily sunscreen prevents new sun damage that accelerates aging. Quality moisturizers keep skin hydrated and plump. Professional treatments like chemical peels or laser therapy complement Botox by addressing texture and tone issues.
Investment in Yourself

Botox in Newport represents an investment in how you present yourself to the world. Pricing varies based on the number of units required for your treatment areas. We charge per unit, and most cosmetic treatments require 20 to 60 units, depending on your anatomy and goals.

Therapeutic Botox for TMJ or chronic headaches may qualify for insurance coverage when medically necessary. Our team handles insurance verification and prior authorization to maximize your benefits. We’ll provide clear pricing information during your consultation so you can make informed decisions.
Botox in Newport

Botox offers proven results with minimal downtime, whether you want to refresh your appearance, relieve jaw pain, or reduce chronic headaches. The treatment’s versatility makes it valuable for both cosmetic and therapeutic concerns—often addressing multiple issues simultaneously.

Newport Family and Cosmetic Dentistry combines medical expertise with an artistic eye to deliver natural, beautiful results. Schedule your consultation today with Dr. Nathan W. Tilman or Dr. Dianne Pannes. Discover how Botox can help you look and feel your best. Call now or book online to get started!

cosmetic-dentistry.title: Cosmetic Dentistry
cosmetic-dentistry.desc: Cosmetic Dentistry improves stained or uneven teeth with veneers, whitening, and bonding for confident smiles today. Visit now!

blocks.x: What Makes a Smile Beautiful?

Beauty lives in the details. Teeth that match in color and shape create visual harmony. The right proportions between your teeth and gums frame your smile perfectly. Even subtle adjustments—like smoothing a rough edge or closing a small gap—can dramatically affect how you perceive your smile.

Everyone’s aesthetic goals differ. Some patients want a complete transformation with multiple procedures. Others simply want to refresh their appearance with teeth whitening or fix one nagging imperfection. Our team listens carefully to your vision and creates a customized treatment plan to achieve your goals within your timeline and budget.
Professional Teeth Whitening That Works

Coffee, tea, red wine, and certain medications leave their mark on your teeth over time. Over-the-counter whitening strips might deliver modest results, but professional whitening at our Newport practice creates the dramatic change you want. We offer both in-office treatments and take-home kits that deliver professional-grade results.

In-office whitening brightens your smile several shades in a single appointment. Dr. Tilman and his team apply a powerful whitening gel and activate it with a special light. You’ll walk out with noticeably whiter teeth in about an hour. Take-home kits give you professional results on your schedule—you’ll wear custom trays filled with whitening gel for a specified time each day until you reach your desired shade.
Porcelain Veneers Transform Smiles

Veneers rank among the most versatile cosmetic dentistry in Newport options. These thin shells of porcelain bond to the front surface of your teeth and can address multiple concerns simultaneously. Chips, cracks, gaps, stains, and minor misalignment all disappear beneath beautifully crafted veneers.
Veneers Can Correct:

The process typically requires two appointments. During the first visit, Dr. Pannes, Dr. May, Dr. Safford, or Dr. Ramos removes a small amount of enamel and takes impressions. Our lab crafts your custom veneers to match your facial features and desired shade. At your second appointment, we bond the veneers in place and make any final adjustments. The results last for years with proper care.
Dental Bonding Fixes Minor Flaws

Not every cosmetic concern requires an extensive procedure. Composite bonding offers a quick, affordable solution for small chips, gaps, or discolored spots. Your dentist applies tooth-colored resin directly to the affected area, sculpts it into the ideal shape, and hardens it with a curing light. The entire process takes less than an hour per tooth in most cases.

Bonding works particularly well for young patients who’ve chipped a tooth during sports or other activities. The conservative approach preserves natural tooth structure while restoring both function and appearance. Many patients can’t tell where their natural tooth ends and the bonding begins.
Smile Makeovers Combine Multiple Treatments

Sometimes, achieving your dream smile requires a comprehensive approach. Smile makeovers blend several cosmetic dentistry procedures into one coordinated treatment plan. For example, you might combine veneers on your front teeth with bonding on adjacent teeth and finish with professional whitening for a completely refreshed appearance.

Dr. Tilman and his colleagues excel at creating these complex treatment plans. When designing your new smile, they consider your facial structure, skin tone, and personal preferences. Digital imaging technology lets you preview potential results before committing to treatment, so you’ll know exactly what to expect.
Gum Contouring Perfects Your Smile Line

Your teeth might look perfect, but if too much gum tissue shows when you smile, you might still feel dissatisfied with your appearance. Gum contouring reshapes your gum line to expose more of your natural teeth. This procedure creates better proportions and can make teeth appear longer and more uniform.

Modern laser technology makes gum contouring more comfortable than ever. The laser removes excess tissue with minimal discomfort and promotes faster healing than traditional methods. Many patients combine gum contouring with veneers for a complete smile transformation.
Maintaining Your Cosmetic Results

Investing in cosmetic dentistry in Newport means investing in yourself, and protecting that investment requires consistent care. Brushing twice daily and flossing once removes plaque before it can stain or damage your enhanced smile. Regular dental cleanings keep your teeth looking bright and help your cosmetic work last longer.

Certain habits threaten your cosmetic results. Chewing ice or hard candies can chip veneers or bonding. Smoking stains teeth and increases your risk of gum disease. Using your teeth as tools—opening packages, biting nails, or tearing tape—puts unnecessary stress on cosmetic restorations. Our team provides detailed care instructions specific to your treatments.
Your Consultation Experience

Choosing cosmetic dentistry feels like a big decision, and our team respects that. Your consultation allows you to express your concerns and ask questions without pressure. We’ll examine your teeth, discuss which procedures would benefit you most, and provide honest recommendations based on our experience.

Cost matters, and we’re upfront about pricing from the start. Many cosmetic procedures represent an investment, but we offer financing options that make treatment accessible. Some procedures even qualify for insurance coverage if they address functional issues alongside cosmetic concerns.
Cosmetic Dentistry in Newport

You don’t have to settle for a smile that makes you hesitate in photos or avoid social situations. Cosmetic dentistry gives you the tools to show the world your best self. Whether you need a minor touch-up or a complete smile makeover, Newport Family and Cosmetic Dentistry has the expertise to bring your vision to life.
We’re proud to serve southern Rhode Island with Newport Family & Cosmetic Dentistry in Newport and Portsmouth Dental Associates in Portsmouth.

Schedule your consultation today with Dr. Nathan W. Tilman, Dr. Dianne Pannes, Dr. Brian May, Dr. James Safford, or Dr. Aaron Ramos. Let’s explore how cosmetic dentistry can transform not just your smile but also your confidence and quality of life. Call our office or book online—your dream smile is closer than you think!

dental-implants.title: Dental Implants
dental-implants.desc: Dental Implants in Newport replace missing teeth with lasting comfort, natural function, and confident smiles today.

blocks.x: How Dental Implants Work

Think of an implant as an artificial tooth root. Your dentist surgically places a titanium post into your jawbone where your natural tooth once lived. Over several months, the bone fuses with the titanium through a process called osseointegration. This creates an incredibly strong foundation that rivals the stability of natural tooth roots.

Once the implant integrates fully, we attach an abutment—a connector piece that links the implant to your final restoration. A custom crown caps the entire system and recreates the visible portion of your tooth. The result feels so natural that most patients forget which teeth are implants within weeks of completing treatment.
Why Choose Implants Over Other Options

Traditional bridges require grinding down healthy adjacent teeth to create support structures. Dentures slip during meals and conversations, creating embarrassing moments. Neither option addresses the bone loss that occurs when tooth roots disappear. Dental implants in Newport solve all these problems simultaneously.
Implants Offer Unique Benefits:

Your jawbone needs stimulation to maintain its density and shape. Natural tooth roots provide this stimulation through daily chewing forces. When you lose a tooth, the surrounding bone begins to shrink—a process that changes your facial structure over time. Implants restore this crucial stimulation and preserve your facial contours.
The Dental Implant Process

Every successful implant begins with thorough planning. Dr. Tilman and his team use advanced 3D imaging to map your jawbone structure and identify the optimal placement position. They evaluate bone density, nerve locations, and sinus proximity to ensure safe, effective placement.

During the surgical appointment, we numb the area completely so you feel only gentle pressure. Your dentist creates a small opening in the gum tissue and prepares the jawbone to receive the implant. The titanium post goes into position, and we close the tissue around it. Most patients experience less discomfort than they anticipated and manage any tenderness with over-the-counter pain relievers.

The healing phase typically lasts three to six months. Your bone gradually grows around the implant threads, creating the solid anchor your restoration needs. You’ll wear a temporary solution during this time so you never go without teeth. Some cases allow for immediate temporary crowns, which Dr. Pannes, Dr. May, Dr. Safford, or Dr. Ramos can discuss during your consultation.
Single Tooth Replacement

Lost one tooth to decay, injury, or extraction? A single dental implant recreates what nature gave you originally. The implant post replaces your root, and a porcelain crown matches your surrounding teeth perfectly. Your new tooth blends seamlessly with your natural smile—friends and family won’t spot the difference.

Single implants protect your remaining teeth by maintaining proper spacing. When a gap exists, adjacent teeth drift into the space over time. This shifting creates bite problems and makes cleaning more difficult. An implant holds everything in proper alignment and distributes chewing forces evenly across your arch.
Multiple Teeth Solutions

Are you missing several teeth in a row? You don’t need one implant for each missing tooth. Strategic implant placement supports a fixed bridge that replaces multiple teeth with just two or three implants. This approach saves time, reduces cost, and delivers outstanding stability.

Implant-supported bridges outperform traditional bridges dramatically. They don’t rely on natural teeth for support, so we preserve healthy tooth structure. You can clean around implants just like natural teeth—no special threaders or awkward techniques required. The solid foundation means you bite confidently and never worry about your restoration loosening.
Full-Arch Restoration

Lost most or all of your teeth? Dental implants in Newport can restore your entire arch with remarkable efficiency. The All-on-4 or All-on-6 technique uses four to six strategically placed implants to support a full-arch of prosthetic teeth. You walk out with a complete, functional smile—often on the same day we place your implants.

Full-arch restoration changes lives. Patients who’ve struggled with failing teeth or uncomfortable dentures suddenly rediscover foods they haven’t enjoyed in years. They speak without slurring or clicking sounds. They smile widely in photos for the first time in decades. The psychological impact matches the functional improvements.
Bone Grafting for Implant Success

Sometimes patients lack sufficient bone density for immediate implant placement. Years of tooth loss, gum disease, or natural bone resorption create challenging situations. Bone grafting procedures solve this problem by building deficient areas with grafting material that stimulates new bone growth.

Dr. Tilman and his colleagues perform bone grafts routinely. The material encourages your body to generate healthy bone tissue over several months. Once adequate bone exists, we proceed with implant placement. While grafting extends your overall treatment timeline, it ensures your implants have the foundation they need for long-term success.
Caring for Your Dental Implants

Implants don’t decay like natural teeth but still require consistent care. Brushing twice daily removes bacteria that can inflame the surrounding gum tissue. Flossing once daily keeps the areas between your implants clean and healthy. Regular professional cleanings allow us to monitor your implants and catch any potential issues early.

Most implant failures occur due to poor oral hygiene or uncontrolled gum disease. Maintain your routine dental visits and follow our home care recommendations. Avoid smoking, which dramatically increases implant failure rates. With proper attention, your implants will serve you faithfully for decades.
Investment in Your Quality of Life

Dental implants represent a significant investment, but consider what you gain. The ability to eat anything you want. Speaking clearly without worrying about slipping dentures. Smiling confidently in every situation. These improvements touch every aspect of daily life.

We offer flexible financing options that make implant treatment accessible. Many dental insurance plans provide partial coverage for implants. Our team handles insurance verification and maximizes your benefits. We’ll provide a clear cost breakdown before beginning treatment so you can make an informed decision.
Dental Implants in Newport

Missing teeth don’t have to define your future. Dental implants give you back everything a natural tooth provides—stability, function, and beauty. Newport Family and Cosmetic Dentistry combines advanced technology with compassionate care to deliver exceptional implant results.
Whether you’re near Newport or Portsmouth, our two locations—Newport Family & Cosmetic Dentistry and Portsmouth Dental Associates—are here for you.

Contact Dr. Nathan W. Tilman, Dr. Dianne Pannes, Dr. Brian May, Dr. James Safford, or Dr. Aaron Ramos to schedule your implant consultation. We’ll evaluate your situation, answer your questions, and create a personalized treatment plan. Your journey toward a complete, confident smile starts with one phone call.

dentures-and-partials.title: Dentures and Partials
dentures-and-partials.desc: Dentures and Partials in Newport restore missing teeth with comfortable, natural-looking solutions. Call today now!

blocks.x: Understanding Your Denture Options

Modern dentistry offers several denture types, each designed for specific situations. Full dentures replace all teeth in an arch when you’ve lost every tooth or need remaining teeth extracted. Partial dentures fill gaps when you still have some healthy, natural teeth worth preserving. Implant-supported dentures attach to dental implants for superior stability and comfort.

The right choice depends on how many teeth you’re missing, your jawbone condition, your budget, and your lifestyle needs. Dr. Tilman and his team evaluate all these factors during your consultation. They explain each option honestly, including advantages and limitations, so you can make an informed decision that serves you well for years.
Full Dentures Replace Complete Arches

Full dentures become necessary when you’ve lost all teeth in your upper or lower arch. These removable prosthetics rest directly on your gum tissue and rely on suction, dental adhesive, or implant attachments for retention. Modern full dentures look remarkably realistic, with teeth shaded to match natural enamel and gums colored to blend with your tissue.

We create conventional full dentures after your gums have healed completely from any extractions. This healing period typically lasts eight to twelve weeks. Your gums and bone reshape significantly during healing, so waiting ensures optimal denture fit. Immediate dentures offer an alternative if you can’t go without teeth during the healing phase.

Immediate dentures get fabricated before your extractions and placed the same day we remove your remaining teeth. You never go without teeth, but these dentures require frequent adjustments as your gums heal and change shape. Most patients eventually need a new set once healing completes, making immediate dentures a temporary solution that bridges you to permanent dentures.
Partial Dentures Fill Multiple Gaps

Still have some healthy natural teeth? Partial dentures replace missing teeth while preserving the good ones you have left. These removable appliances attach to your remaining teeth with metal clasps or precision attachments. They prevent your existing teeth from shifting into empty spaces while restoring your ability to chew effectively.
Partial Dentures Work Well When:

Quality partial dentures in Newport blend seamlessly with your natural teeth. We match shade and shape carefully so the replacement teeth look like they belong in your smile. The base material that holds artificial teeth and rests against your gums can be made from acrylic or a flexible thermoplastic material that many patients find more comfortable.
Implant-Supported Dentures Transform Lives

Traditional dentures slip during eating and speaking, requiring messy adhesives that don’t always work. Implant-supported dentures solve these frustrations by attaching to dental implants surgically placed in your jawbone. The stability feels incredible—like having your natural teeth back.

Several implants placed strategically in your jaw create anchor points for your denture. Some implant dentures snap on and off for easy cleaning. Others get screwed into place by your dentist and function like permanent teeth. Both approaches deliver far superior stability compared to traditional dentures.

The bone stimulation from implants prevents the jawbone deterioration that occurs with conventional dentures. Your facial structure maintains its shape instead of gradually collapsing inward. This preservation keeps you looking younger and ensures your denture fits well years later.
The Denture Creation Process

Creating quality dentures requires multiple appointments and careful attention to detail. Your first visit involves a comprehensive examination, impressions, and a discussion of your goals. Dr. Pannes, Dr. May, Dr. Safford, and Dr. Ramos evaluate your oral health and determine whether teeth need extraction before denture fabrication begins.

We take precise impressions of your gums and any remaining teeth. These impressions capture every contour and detail necessary for optimal denture fit. Bite registration records document how your jaws relate to each other, ensuring your new teeth align properly for comfortable chewing.

At your next appointment, you’ll try in a wax model of your denture. This try-in stage lets you see how your dentures will look and allows us to make adjustments before final fabrication. We evaluate tooth position, shade, shape, and bite alignment. Your input matters—this is your smile, and we want you thrilled with the results.

The dental lab crafts your final dentures based on the approved wax try-in. We place your completed dentures at your delivery appointment and make any necessary adjustments for comfort and fit. We’ll teach you proper insertion, removal, and cleaning techniques. Follow-up appointments ensure everything works perfectly as you adapt to your new teeth.
Adjusting to New Dentures

Wearing dentures feels strange initially—something foreign sitting in your mouth where teeth used to be. This awkwardness fades as your mouth adapts. Speaking might sound different at first, and certain words prove challenging. Reading aloud at home helps you adjust faster. Within a few weeks, speaking feels natural again.

Eating requires some adjustment, too. Start with soft foods cut into small pieces. Chew slowly using both sides of your mouth simultaneously to keep dentures stable. Avoid sticky or very hard foods during your adjustment period. As you gain confidence, you’ll expand your diet and find you can eat most foods comfortably.

Increased salivation occurs during the first few days as your mouth responds to the new appliance. This excess saliva production decreases quickly. Some minor soreness might develop where dentures contact your gums. These sore spots require adjustment—contact our office rather than suffering through discomfort.
Denture Care and Maintenance

Dentures require daily cleaning just like natural teeth. Remove them after meals and rinse away food particles. Brush your dentures daily with a soft-bristled brush and denture cleaner—never regular toothpaste, which scratches the surface. Handle them carefully over a towel or water-filled sink since dropping them can cause cracks or breaks.

Remove dentures at night to rest your gum tissues. Soak them in water or a denture solution overnight to prevent warping. Never use hot water, as it can distort the denture material. Clean your gums, tongue, and natural teeth with a soft brush before reinserting dentures each morning.

Your dentures need professional evaluation annually, even if they seem fine. Dr. Tilman and his colleagues check for wear, damage, and fit issues. As bone and gums remodel, your mouth changes over time, so periodic adjustments maintain optimal comfort. Most dentures last five to seven years before replacement becomes necessary.
When Dentures Need Relining

Over time, your jawbone and gums change shape. Dentures that fit perfectly at delivery might feel loose a year or two later. Relining adds new base material to your existing dentures, improving fit without requiring complete replacement. This process costs less than new dentures and extends the life of your current set.

Soft relines use pliable material that cushions the gums—ideal for patients with sensitive tissues or significant bone loss. Hard relines use durable acrylic that lasts longer. We evaluate which approach best suits your situation. Most dentures need relining every two to three years to maintain proper fit and function.
Cost Considerations and Insurance

Dentures represent a significant investment in your quality of life. Full dentures typically cost less than extensive implant work but more than a single crown. Partial dentures fall somewhere in between, depending on complexity. Implant-supported dentures cost more initially but deliver superior function and longevity.

Most dental insurance plans provide partial coverage for dentures since they’re considered necessary for oral function. Coverage percentages and annual maximums vary by plan. Our team verifies your benefits and handles insurance claims to maximize your coverage. We also offer financing options that make quality dentures accessible even if insurance doesn’t cover the full cost.
Dentures in Newport

Missing teeth don’t have to limit your life any longer. Dentures restore your ability to eat comfortably, speak clearly, and smile confidently. Whether you need a full set, partial denture, or implant-supported option, our experienced team creates comfortable, natural-looking solutions tailored to your needs.

Schedule your denture consultation today with Dr. Nathan W. Tilman, Dr. Dianne Pannes, Dr. Brian May, Dr. James Safford, or Dr. Aaron Ramos. Newport Family and Cosmetic Dentistry will evaluate your situation, discuss all options, and develop a treatment plan that returns your complete smile. Contact us now to get started!
From Newport Family & Cosmetic Dentistry in Newport to Portsmouth Dental Associates in Portsmouth, our patients enjoy exceptional service.

emergency-visits.title: Emergency Visits
emergency-visits.desc: Get prompt treatment for dental emergencies in Newport and Portsmouth, RI. Call for urgent care today.

blocks.x: Dr. Nathan W. Tilman, Dr. Dianne Pannes, Dr. Brian May, Dr. James Safford, and Dr. Aaron Ramos prioritize dental emergencies because they know pain and trauma can’t wait. Their quick response and skilled treatment get you out of pain and protect your oral health from further damage.
Experience compassionate care at Newport Family & Cosmetic Dentistry (Newport) and Portsmouth Dental Associates (Portsmouth)—two convenient locations under one trusted team.
Common Dental Emergencies

Dental emergencies take many forms, but they all share one thing—they require immediate attention. Severe toothaches that keep you awake at night signal infection or decay reaching your tooth’s nerve. Knocked-out teeth need reimplantation within an hour for the best chance of survival. Broken or cracked teeth expose sensitive inner layers and create sharp edges that cut your tongue and cheeks.

Lost fillings or crowns leave teeth vulnerable to further damage and temperature sensitivity. Abscesses—pus-filled infections in your gums or at tooth roots—can spread to other parts of your body if left untreated. Soft tissue injuries like cut lips, torn cheeks, or punctured tongues bleed heavily and risk infection. Orthodontic emergencies like broken wires can poke and irritate your mouth. All these situations demand quick professional care.
When to Seek Emergency Dental Care

Some dental problems feel urgent but can wait until regular business hours. Others require immediate attention to save your tooth or prevent serious complications. Understanding the difference helps you respond appropriately when problems arise.
Seek Immediate Emergency Care For:

Less urgent issues like minor chips without pain, small pieces of broken fillings, or lost temporary crowns can typically wait a day or two. Call our office to describe your situation—we’ll tell you honestly whether you need same-day treatment or can schedule a regular appointment.
First Aid Before You Reach Us

Taking the right steps before arriving for emergency dentistry in Newport can make a significant difference in treatment outcomes. For knocked-out teeth, time matters critically. Pick up the tooth by its crown—never touch the root. Rinse it gently with water if dirty, but don’t scrub or remove attached tissue. Try to place the tooth back in its socket if possible, or keep it moist in milk or saliva while you rush to our office.

Broken teeth benefit from rinsing your mouth with warm water and applying a cold compress to reduce swelling. Save any tooth fragments you find—we might use them in your repair. For severe bleeding, apply firm pressure with clean gauze for 10 minutes. Keep applying pressure until bleeding stops or you reach our office.

Toothaches often respond temporarily to over-the-counter pain relievers and cold compresses applied to your cheek. Avoid placing aspirin directly on your gums—it burns tissue. Never apply heat to dental pain as it can worsen infections. These measures buy you some relief until Dr. Tilman or his colleagues can address the underlying problem.
What to Expect During Your Emergency Visit

We understand dental emergencies create stress and anxiety. Our team works quickly to assess your situation and provide relief. Your emergency appointment begins with describing what happened and when your symptoms started. We’ll examine the affected area thoroughly and take X-rays if needed to understand the full extent of damage or infection.

Pain relief comes first. Dr. Pannes, Dr. May, Dr. Safford, or Dr. Ramos will numb the area and may prescribe medication for pain and inflammation. Once you’re comfortable, we’ll explain what happened and discuss treatment options. Some emergencies get resolved completely in one visit—re-cementing a crown, treating an infection, or repairing a simple fracture.

Complex cases might require temporary stabilization during your emergency visit with definitive treatment scheduled for a follow-up appointment. For example, a tooth needing root canal treatment might receive initial therapy to eliminate infection, with final restoration completed later. We’ll never leave you in pain or with unstable dental work.
Preventing Dental Emergencies

While accidents happen, many dental emergencies stem from preventable causes. Regular dental checkups catch small problems before they become painful emergencies. That small cavity detected early gets a simple filling, but ignored, it progresses until the tooth breaks or infection develops, requiring emergency treatment.

Wear a custom mouthguard during sports—they protect against knocked-out and broken teeth far better than generic store-bought versions. Address teeth grinding with a nightguard before you crack teeth from excessive force. Don’t use your teeth as tools to open packages, hold objects, or crack nuts. These habits cause fractures that land you in the emergency chair.

Pay attention to warning signs your body sends. Persistent sensitivity to hot or cold, gums that bleed regularly, or dull aching that comes and goes all signal developing problems. Addressing these issues proactively prevents middle-of-the-night pain that demands emergency care.
Insurance and Payment for Emergencies

Most dental insurance plans cover emergency treatment similarly to regular procedures. We’ll verify your coverage and handle insurance claims to maximize your benefits. If you lack insurance, don’t let cost prevent you from seeking necessary care. Dental infections can become life-threatening if they spread, and knocked-out teeth lose viability with each passing hour.

We offer flexible payment options and financing to make emergency care accessible. Discuss your concerns openly—we’ll work with you to ensure you receive the treatment you need. Your health and safety matter more than payment arrangements, and we’ll find a solution that works.
After Emergency Treatment

Follow all post-treatment instructions carefully to ensure proper healing. Take prescribed medications as directed—finish the entire course of antibiotics even if you feel better. Stick to soft foods if instructed and avoid chewing on the treated side. Maintain gentle oral hygiene around the affected area, but don’t skip brushing and flossing elsewhere.

Schedule any recommended follow-up appointments promptly. Emergency treatment often serves as the first phase of care, with additional visits needed to complete restoration. Missing these appointments risks treatment failure and recurrence of problems.

Watch for warning signs that healing isn’t progressing normally—increasing pain, swelling, fever, or discharge. Contact our office immediately if these symptoms develop. Most emergency dental work heals predictably, but catching complications early prevents serious problems.
Emergency Dentistry in Newport

Dental emergencies create stress, pain, and worry. You shouldn’t face these situations alone or struggle to find care. Emergency dentistry means having a skilled team ready to help when urgent problems arise. We reserve appointment slots for same-day emergencies and respond quickly to calls from patients in distress.

Don’t suffer through dental pain or risk permanent damage by delaying treatment. Contact Newport Family and Cosmetic Dentistry immediately when dental emergencies strike. Dr. Nathan W. Tilman, Dr. Dianne Pannes, Dr. Brian May, Dr. James Safford, and Dr. Aaron Ramos will see you promptly and provide the expert care you need. Call our office now—we’re ready to help!

orthodontics.title: Orthodontics
orthodontics.desc: Achieve a healthier, straighter smile with personalized orthodontic treatment in Newport and Portsmouth.

blocks.x: Whether you’re a parent researching options for your teenager or an adult finally ready to address those crooked teeth you’ve lived with for decades, you’re in the right place. Our practice combines cutting-edge technology with the kind of personalized attention that makes dental visits actually pleasant.
For personalized treatment, visit one of our two locations: Newport Family & Cosmetic Dentistry (Newport) or Portsmouth Dental Associates (Portsmouth).
Why Adults and Teens Choose Orthodontic Treatment

You might think orthodontics only matters for cosmetic reasons, but straight teeth do so much more than look good in photos. When your bite aligns properly, you reduce wear on your tooth enamel. You make brushing and flossing easier, which means fewer cavities down the road. Some patients even notice their jaw pain disappears once their teeth sit in the right position.

Dr. Dianne Pannes has worked with countless patients who waited years to pursue treatment, thinking they’d missed their window. Here’s the truth: your teeth can move at any age. Adults often make excellent orthodontic candidates because they’re motivated and consistent with their treatment plans.
SureSmile Clear Aligners: The Modern Approach

Traditional braces work wonderfully for many cases, but clear aligners have revolutionized how we approach orthodontics. SureSmile represents one of the most advanced systems available today. These custom-made aligners gradually shift your teeth into their ideal positions using precise 3D imaging and treatment planning.
What makes SureSmile different:
Dr. Brian May uses digital scans to map out your entire treatment journey before you even start. You’ll see a preview of your future smile, which feels real and exciting rather than daunting.
Your First Orthodontic Consultation

Walking into an orthodontic consultation can feel intimidating if you don’t know what to expect. Dr. James Safford makes this process straightforward and pressure-free. During your initial visit, we’ll take detailed images of your teeth and jaw and discuss what bothers you about your current smile and what you hope to achieve.

Some people need minor corrections that take just a few months. Others require more comprehensive treatment spanning a year or longer. We’ll explain exactly what your situation requires and give you realistic timelines. No surprises, no upselling treatments you don’t need.
Orthodontics for the Whole Family

One of the best parts about offering orthodontics in Newport at a family practice is that we can treat multiple generations under one roof. Dr. Aaron Ramos loves seeing siblings go through treatment together or parents and teenagers working toward their smile goals simultaneously.

Early orthodontic evaluations for children help us catch potential issues before they become complicated. Sometimes, we recommend a two-phase approach: We do minor interventions while kids still have baby teeth and complete treatment once their adult teeth come in. Other times, the best strategy involves waiting until their jaw growth stabilizes.
Life During Orthodontic Treatment

Your daily routine shifts slightly during treatment, but most patients adapt quickly. With SureSmile aligners, you wear them 20-22 hours per day, removing them only for meals and brushing. As your teeth gradually move, you switch to a new set of aligners every week or two.

Some mild soreness happens when you start a new aligner, but it typically fades within a day or two. This discomfort actually signals that your treatment works. Your teeth are shifting into better positions, even if you can’t see dramatic changes yet.
Regular checkups let us track your progress and make any necessary adjustments. These visits usually take less time than you’d spend at appointments with traditional braces.
The Investment in Your Smile

Cost concerns keep many people from pursuing orthodontic treatment, but financing options make care accessible for most budgets. We work with various payment plans and accept most dental insurance policies. Many insurance plans cover a portion of orthodontic treatment, even for adults.

Think of orthodontics as an investment that pays dividends for decades. Straighter teeth often mean fewer dental problems later, saving money over your lifetime. Plus, the confidence boost that comes with loving your smile? That’s genuinely priceless.
Orthodontics in Newport

Orthodontics doesn’t have to feel overwhelming or out of reach. Dr. Nathan W. Tilman, Dr. Dianne Pannes, Dr. Brian May, Dr. James Safford, and Dr. Aaron Ramos have helped hundreds of patients achieve the smiles they’ve always wanted. We’d love to help you determine whether clear aligners or another orthodontic option fits your needs.

Schedule your consultation with Newport Family and Cosmetic Dentistry today. Visit us at smile-newport.com or give us a call. Your future smile is waiting, and we’ll be with you every step of the way to make it happen.

periodontal-treatment-gum-care.title: Periodontal Treatment (Gum Care)
periodontal-treatment-gum-care.desc: Protect your oral health with expert gum disease treatment and periodontal care in Newport and Portsmouth.

blocks.x: For Non-Surgical Perio and Routine Cleanings

Our office provides specialized care for Non-Surgical Periodontal Treatments—including Scaling and Root Planing (SRPs) and Perio Maintenance—and Routine Cleanings (Prophy appointments). Each treatment is tailored to your unique dental health needs and designed to maintain optimal gum and tooth health.

In addition, our office proudly offers Guided Biofilm Therapy (GBT) — an advanced, gentle, and highly effective approach to removing plaque and biofilm. GBT combines state-of-the-art technology with a comfortable, minimally invasive process that promotes healthier gums and cleaner teeth. It is suitable for both periodontal patients and those receiving routine dental cleanings.
Understanding Gum Disease

Gum disease starts when plaque and bacteria accumulate along your gum line. This sticky film hardens into tartar that irritates gum tissue and triggers inflammation. Early gum disease, called gingivitis, causes swollen, red gums that bleed easily. Left untreated, it advances to periodontitis—a serious infection that destroys the bone supporting your teeth.

Periodontitis creates pockets between teeth and gums where bacteria thrive. These pockets deepen as infection progresses, loosening teeth and eventually causing tooth loss. The bacteria don’t stay confined to your mouth, either. Research links gum disease to heart disease, diabetes complications, stroke, and respiratory problems. Treating gum disease protects both your oral health and your overall well-being.
Scaling and Root Planing (SRP) Explained

Scaling and root planing—often called a deep cleaning—goes beyond routine dental cleanings. This therapeutic procedure removes tartar and bacteria from below the gum line, where regular cleaning can’t reach. The treatment involves two key steps that work together to restore gum health.

Scaling removes tartar deposits from tooth surfaces and beneath the gum line. Your dental professional uses specialized instruments to break up and remove hardened buildup. Ultrasonic scalers make the process faster and more comfortable by vibrating tartar loose while flushing the area with water. Hand instruments allow precise removal in tight spaces and ensure thorough cleaning.

Root planing smooths rough spots on tooth roots where bacteria love to collect. This smoothing helps your gums reattach to clean tooth surfaces. The procedure also removes infected tissue and bacterial toxins embedded in root surfaces. Together, scaling and root planing eliminate the infection source and create conditions for healing.

Our team offers the option of local anesthesia to ensure patient comfort during procedures such as scaling and root planing. Our doctors and licensed dental hygienists are trained and certified to administer local anesthesia safely when necessary. This helps minimize discomfort and allows patients to undergo treatment easily and confidently.
Guided Biofilm Therapy (GBT)

Guided Biofilm Therapy (GBT) represents the latest advancement in professional teeth cleaning. Using AirFlow® technology, it gently removes biofilm, stains, and soft deposits with a fine powder and warm water spray. GBT is safe for natural teeth, implants, and restorations and is ideal for both periodontal maintenance and routine cleanings. This technology ensures a more comfortable, efficient, and precise cleaning experience.

GBT also helps reduce bacterial load and inflammation, promoting long-term gum health while minimizing discomfort compared to traditional methods. Patients often find it a more pleasant and less invasive alternative.
Healing and Follow-Up Care

Your gums begin healing immediately after treatment. The inflammation decreases, pockets shrink, and tissues reattach to clean tooth surfaces. Complete healing takes several weeks as your body repairs damaged tissue and eliminates remaining bacteria.

We schedule a follow-up appointment about four to six weeks after completing treatment. Dr. Tilman and his colleagues measure pocket depths again to assess healing progress. Successfully treated pockets shrink to healthy depths. Any areas that don’t respond adequately might require additional treatment or referral to a periodontist.
Maintaining Your Results

Scaling, root planing, GBT, and regular maintenance visits treat existing disease and prevent recurrence. Brush twice daily with a soft-bristle brush and fluoride toothpaste. Spend at least two minutes covering all tooth surfaces. Floss once daily to remove plaque between teeth, where gum disease often starts.

Many patients benefit from professional cleanings every three to four months rather than the standard six-month interval. These maintenance visits—especially when using GBT—help prevent new tartar buildup and detect disease recurrence early.
Non-Surgical Periodontal Treatment in Newport, RI

Gum disease threatens your smile, health, and quality of life. The good news? Effective, gentle treatments exist. Whether you need Scaling and Root Planing, Perio Maintenance, or Routine Cleanings enhanced with Guided Biofilm Therapy, Newport Family and Cosmetic Dentistry provides comprehensive, comfortable care for every stage of gum health.

Schedule your non-surgical periodontal treatment in Newport today with Dr. Nathan W. Tilman, Dr. Dianne Pannes, Dr. Brian May, Dr. James Safford, or Dr. Aaron Ramos. Let us help you maintain a healthy, radiant smile with the latest in preventive and therapeutic care.

sleep-apnea-treatment.title: Sleep Apnea Treatment
sleep-apnea-treatment.desc: Improve sleep and overall health with custom sleep apnea treatment in Newport and Portsmouth, RI.

blocks.x: Signs You Might Have Sleep Apnea

Loud, chronic snoring serves as the most obvious warning sign. Your bed partner might report that you stop breathing for several seconds, then gasp or choke before resuming normal breathing. These pauses terrify loved ones who witness them regularly.
Common Sleep Apnea Symptoms Include:

Many people dismiss these symptoms as normal signs of aging or stress. They push through exhaustion with extra coffee and adapt to feeling subpar. Sleep apnea treatment in Newport addresses the root cause rather than masking symptoms, delivering energy and mental clarity you haven’t experienced in years.
Oral Appliance Therapy Explained

CPAP machines remain the most recognized sleep apnea treatment, but many patients struggle with these devices. The mask feels claustrophobic, the noise disturbs sleep, and the equipment makes travel cumbersome. Oral appliance therapy offers an effective alternative that many patients find much more comfortable and convenient.

Custom oral appliances look similar to athletic mouthguards or orthodontic retainers. You wear the device during sleep, and it gently repositions your lower jaw forward. This forward position opens your airway, preventing the soft tissue collapse that causes breathing interruptions. The appliance maintains this optimal jaw position throughout the night without conscious effort on your part.

We create these appliances specifically for your mouth structure. We take detailed impressions and measurements to ensure proper fit and effective positioning. The custom fabrication makes these devices far more comfortable and successful than generic “boil and bite” appliances sold online.
Benefits of Oral Appliance Therapy

Oral appliances deliver impressive results for mild to moderate sleep apnea cases. Even some severe cases respond well, particularly when patients cannot tolerate CPAP therapy. The devices prove especially effective for people whose sleep apnea worsens when sleeping on their backs.

Convenience ranks among the biggest advantages. The appliance fits in a small case that travels anywhere. No electrical outlet required, no distilled water to pack, no filters to replace. You simply insert it before bed and remove it upon waking. Cleaning takes seconds with a toothbrush and water.

Partners appreciate oral appliances too. The devices eliminate or dramatically reduce snoring, creating peaceful sleep for everyone in the bedroom. Couples who’ve spent years in separate rooms often reunite once treatment resolves the snoring issue. The psychological benefits of sharing a bed again strengthen relationships significantly.
Getting Started with Treatment

Sleep apnea treatment in Newport begins with proper diagnosis. We can refer you to a qualified sleep physician if you haven’t had a sleep study yet. Diagnosis requires an overnight sleep study in a lab or a home sleep test. These tests measure your breathing patterns, oxygen levels, heart rate, and other vital parameters throughout the night.

Once diagnosed, you’ll return to Newport Family and Cosmetic Dentistry to discuss oral appliance options. Dr. Pannes will examine your mouth, jaw, and teeth to confirm you’re a good candidate. Certain dental conditions or jaw problems might affect appliance success, and we’ll address any concerns before proceeding.

We’ll take precise impressions of your teeth and bite registration records. These go to a specialized dental lab that fabricates oral appliances. When your custom device returns, you’ll have a fitting appointment, during which we adjust it for optimal comfort and effectiveness and teach you proper insertion, removal, and cleaning techniques.
Adjusting to Your Oral Appliance

Most patients adapt to their oral appliances within a few nights. You might notice some initial jaw tenderness or excessive salivation, but these effects resolve quickly. Your dentist will schedule follow-up appointments to assess how well the device works and make any necessary adjustments.

Communication with your sleep physician remains important throughout treatment. A follow-up sleep study while wearing your oral appliance confirms its effectiveness. We’ll collaborate with your doctor to ensure your treatment achieves optimal results. Some patients benefit from combination therapy using an oral appliance and CPAP at a lower pressure setting.
Sleep Apnea Treatment in Newport

Living with untreated sleep apnea robs you of energy, health, and quality of life. You deserve restful nights and energetic days. Sleep apnea treatment with oral appliance therapy offers a comfortable, convenient solution that fits your lifestyle while delivering the desired results.

Contact Newport Family and Cosmetic Dentistry to schedule your consultation with Dr. Nathan Tilman or Dr. Dianne Pannes. Our team will evaluate your needs, answer your questions, and develop a treatment plan that helps you sleep soundly again. Better sleep starts with one phone call—reach out today!

technology.title: Technology
technology.desc: Experience precise, comfortable care with advanced dental technology, digital imaging, and CEREC crowns.

blocks.x: How Advanced Technology Shapes Better Dental Care

Every tool in our office has a purpose: to enhance accuracy, safety, and comfort. Digital dentistry lets us diagnose earlier, plan treatments more precisely, and complete procedures with minimal disruption to your day. We’re not just using gadgets—we’re using them to improve your experience from start to finish.
CBCT Imaging and Digital X-rays

Traditional X-rays gave us a snapshot; CBCT and digital radiography give us a full story. CBCT (Cone Beam Computed Tomography) provides 3D images of your teeth, jaw, and bone structure with remarkable clarity. We can analyze even the tiniest details—vital for dental implants, root canals, and complex cases. Digital X-rays reduce radiation exposure while delivering instant results that help us explain your treatment plan clearly.
Intraoral Cameras and Scanning

Transparency matters. With our intraoral cameras and high-resolution scanners, you can see exactly what we see. Instead of guessing or relying on abstract descriptions, you’ll view detailed images of your teeth and gums on-screen. These tools also make digital impressions smoother, eliminating the need for uncomfortable trays and molds.
Technology in Newport Restorations: Precision Meets Comfort

CEREC technology allows us to craft crowns, veneers, and onlays right here in the office—often in a single visit. No waiting for lab work, no temporary crowns, no follow-up hassles. Using digital scanning and 3D printing, we design your restoration to blend perfectly with your natural teeth.

3D printing isn’t just for restorations—it also helps us produce precise surgical guides for implants and orthodontic models that make treatment more predictable and efficient. Every design comes from a digital blueprint customized for your smile.
Enhancing Hygiene and Comfort

Preventive care thrives on precision, too. With Guided Biofilm Therapy (GBT), we use airflow and gentle powders to remove plaque and bacteria without the discomfort of scraping. It’s ideal for sensitive patients and those with dental implants. You’ll leave with cleaner teeth and healthier gums—without the post-appointment soreness.

We also integrate Oraverse, a local anesthesia reversal agent, to help you regain normal sensation faster after treatment. It’s a small touch that makes a big difference, especially when you have a busy day ahead.
Digital Coordination for Seamless Care

Our use of Electronic Health Records ensures your dental history, imaging, and treatment plans stay accurate and easily accessible. This integration allows us to coordinate care between our two locations—Newport Family & Cosmetic Dentistry in Newport and Portsmouth Dental Associates in Portsmouth—with complete consistency. Whether you’re here for a routine checkup or a cosmetic enhancement, every detail is securely documented and available for your comfort and safety.
Discover a New Standard of Dental Care

At our Newport and Portsmouth offices, we believe dental technology should simplify life, not complicate it. Our technology in Newport does that. By investing in the latest tools and training, we’ve created a space where innovation meets warmth—where your smile feels like the priority it truly is.
If you’re ready to experience modern dentistry that feels personal, schedule your visit today. Let’s create the healthy, confident smile you’ve been waiting for.

dental-insurance.title: Dental Insurance
dental-insurance.desc: Learn about dental insurance options, coverage, and payment solutions at our Newport and Portsmouth offices.

blocks.x: Financing Your Care

At Newport Family and Cosmetic Dentistry and Portsmouth Dental Associates, we want your dental care to be as smooth and stress-free as possible. Dental insurance can be confusing, and coverage varies based on the plan your employer has selected. For details about your benefits, please review the insurance booklet provided by your employer.

We will give you an estimate of your insurance coverage before treatment based on the information received from your insurance company. Please note that this is only an estimate, not a guarantee of payment.

As a courtesy, we file all insurance claims for you. However, any balance not covered by your insurance remains your responsibility. If your insurance pays more or less than expected, we will adjust your account accordingly.
If you have questions about your coverage, our financial policies, or financing options, our team is always happy to help.
Payment options:
Other options:
Please note: Payment is due at the time of service.

As a courtesy to you, we will bill your insurance company and track claims. Please keep us informed of any changes to your insurance plan. You are responsible for the fees charged by our office, no matter what your insurance coverage may be. Most insurance companies should respond to the claim within four to six weeks. Any remaining cost is your responsibility.
We are here to help! Please call for more information about financing your oral health needs in our office!

financial-policy.title: Financial Policy
financial-policy.desc: Review payment options, financing information, and office policies for your dental care needs.

blocks.x: Patient Billing
Please remember you are fully responsible for all fees charged by this office, regardless of your insurance coverage.

We will send you a monthly statement. Most insurance companies will respond within four to six weeks. Please call our office if your statement does not reflect your insurance payment within that time frame. Any remaining balance after your insurance has paid is your responsibility. Your prompt remittance is appreciated. We can make arrangements for a monthly payment plan, but this must be done prior to the actual procedure.

home-care-instructions.title: Home Care Instructions
home-care-instructions.desc: Access helpful home care instructions to support healing and maintain a healthy smile after treatment.

blocks.x: It is normal to experience some hot and cold sensitivity. The teeth require some time to heal after the removal of tooth structure and will be sensitive in the interim. Your gums may also be sore for a few days. Warm salt water rinses (a teaspoon of salt in a cup of warm water) three times a day will reduce pain and swelling. A mild pain medication (one tablet of Tylenol or Ibuprofen (Motrin) every 3-4 hours) should ease any residual discomfort.

Don’t be concerned if your speech is affected for the first few days. You’ll quickly adapt and be speaking normally. You may notice increased salivation. This is because your brain is responding to the new size and shape of your teeth. This should subside to normal in about a week.
Daily brushing and flossing are a must for your new dental work. Daily plaque removal is critical for the long-term success of your new teeth, as are regular cleaning appointments.

Any food that can crack, chip, or damage a natural tooth can do the same to your new teeth. Avoid hard foods and substances (such as beer nuts, peanut brittle, ice, fingernails, or pencils) and sticky candies. Smoking will stain your new teeth. Minimize or avoid foods that stain, such as coffee, red wine, tea, and berries.

If you engage in sports, let us know so we can make a custom mouthguard. If you grind your teeth at night, wear the night guard we have provided for you. Adjusting to the look and feel of your new smile will take time. If you have any problems or concerns, please let us know. We always welcome your questions.
After Crown and Bridge Appointments

Dental crowns and dental bridges usually take two or three appointments to complete. In the first visit, the teeth are prepared, and molds of the mouth are taken. Temporary crowns or bridges are placed to protect the teeth while the custom restoration is being made. Since the teeth will be anesthetized, the tongue, lips, and roof of the mouth may be numb. Please refrain from eating and drinking hot beverages until the numbness is completely worn off.

Occasionally, a temporary crown may come off. Call us at (401) 846-3801 if this happens, and bring the temporary crown with you so we can re-cement it. It is very important for the temporary to stay in place, as it will prevent other teeth from moving and compromising the fit of your final restoration.

To keep your temporaries in place, avoid eating sticky foods (gum), hard foods, and, if possible, chew on the opposite side of your mouth. It is important to brush normally, but floss carefully and don’t pull up on the floss, which may dislodge the temporary, but pull the floss out from the side of the temporary crown.

It is normal to experience some temperature and pressure sensitivity after each appointment. The sensitivity should subside a few weeks after the placement of the final restoration. Mild pain medications may also be used as directed by our office.
If your bite feels uneven, if you have persistent pain, or if you have any other questions or concerns, please call our office at (401) 846-3801.
After Tooth Extraction

After tooth extraction, it’s important for a blood clot to form to stop the bleeding and begin the healing process. That’s why we ask you to bite on a gauze pad for 30-45 minutes after the appointment. If the bleeding or oozing still persists, place another gauze pad and bite firmly for another 30 minutes. You may have to do this several times.

After the blood clot forms, it is important not to disturb or dislodge the clot as it aids healing. Do not rinse vigorously, suck on straws, smoke, drink alcohol, or brush teeth next to the extraction site for 72 hours. These activities will dislodge or dissolve the clot and retard the healing process. Limit vigorous exercise for the next 24 hours, as this will increase blood pressure and may cause more bleeding from the extraction site.

After the tooth is extracted, you may feel some pain and experience some swelling. An ice pack or an unopened bag of frozen peas or corn applied to the area will keep swelling to a minimum. Take pain medications as prescribed. The swelling usually subsides after 48 hours.

Use the pain medication as directed. Call the office at (401) 846-3801 if the medication doesn’t seem to be working. If antibiotics are prescribed, continue to take them for the indicated length of time, even if signs and symptoms of infection are gone. Drink lots of fluid and eat nutritious soft food on the day of the extraction. You can eat normally as soon as you are comfortable.

It is important to resume your normal dental routine after 24 hours. This should include brushing and flossing your teeth at least once a day. This will speed healing and help keep your mouth fresh and clean.

After a few days, you will feel fine and can resume your normal activities. If you have heavy bleeding, severe pain, continued swelling for 2-3 days, or a reaction to the medication, call our office immediately at (401) 846-3801.
After Composite Fillings (White Fillings)

When an anesthetic has been used, your lips and tongue may be numb for several hours after the appointment. Avoid any chewing and hot beverages until the numbness has completely worn off. It is very easy to bite or burn your tongue or lip while you are numb.

It is normal to experience some hot, cold & pressure sensitivity after your dental filling appointment. Injection sites may also be sore. Ibuprofen (Motrin), Tylenol, or aspirin (one tablet every 3-4 hours as needed for pain) work well to alleviate the tenderness. If pressure sensitivity persists beyond a few days or if the sensitivity to hot or cold increases, contact our office at (401) 846-3801.
You may chew with your composite fillings as soon as the anesthetic completely wears off, since they are fully set when you leave the office.
If your bite feels uneven, if you have persistent pain, or if you have any other questions or concerns, please call our office at (401) 846-3801.

dental-anxiety-phobia.title: Dental Anxiety & Phobia
dental-anxiety-phobia.desc: Feel comfortable at every visit with compassionate care for dental anxiety and phobia in Newport and Portsmouth.

blocks.x: Dental Anxiety is Common
Dental Anxiety vs. Dental Phobia

Dental anxieties and phobias present themselves in a wide variety of ways, and specific fears vary from person to person. Dental anxiety may be mild to moderate, and often takes the form of a general sense of worry and apprehension when thinking about an upcoming procedure. Dental phobia is a more intense experience, with patients feeling an overwhelming, irrational fear of dental work. This can sometimes cause them to avoid care altogether.
We Can Help!

Whatever your individual level of anxiety may be, our office is committed to making sure that your visit is as comfortable, quick, and easy as possible. There are many solutions for dental anxiety, and we are ready to help.
Here are a few popular methods for easing anxiety in the dental office:

Oral health is important for the health of your whole body. Don’t let fear stand in your way of good dental care! We can help you get the care that you need. Call Today to Make an Appointment. (401) 846-3801.

nitrous-oxide.title: Nitrous Oxide
nitrous-oxide.desc: Relax during treatment with safe, effective nitrous oxide sedation in Newport and Portsmouth, RI.

blocks.x: Laughing Gas
Nitrous Oxide is a sweet-smelling, non-irritating, colorless gas that you can breathe.
Nitrous Oxide has been the primary means of sedation in dentistry for many years. Nitrous oxide is safe; the patient receives 50-70% oxygen with no less than 30% nitrous oxide.
The patient is able to breathe on their own and remain in control of all bodily functions.
The patient may experience mild amnesia and may fall asleep, not remembering all of what happened during their appointment.
There are many advantages to using Nitrous Oxide
Reasons to not use Nitrous Oxide

You should not utilize Nitrous Oxide if you have been diagnosed with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). Though there are no other major contraindications to using nitrous oxide, you may not want to use it if you have emphysema, exotic chest problems, M.S., a cold, or other difficulties with breathing.
You may want to ask your dentist for a “5-minute trial” to see how you feel with this type of sedation method before proceeding.
